feedback.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.

paragraphs.text: Mynet
Uso pubblico

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Introductory Note
paragraphs.text: Mynet is one of Italy's largest independent telecommunications companies, with over 32,000 km of proprietary fiber-optic infrastructure and a strong presence across Northern and Central Italy. Founded with the mission to connect even the most underserved areas, Mynet has been a pioneer in bringing high-speed broadband to rural towns, industrial districts, and remote communities, where large incumbents typically fail to invest.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Introductory Note
paragraphs.text: Through a vertically integrated model and a strong territorial footprint, Mynet delivers ultrabroadband fiber services, wireless access, data center hosting and cloud solutions to both private users and businesses, including SMEs and public administrations. Mynet operates without public subsidies, investing its own capital and prioritizing technical excellence, network resilience, and local trust.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Introductory Note
paragraphs.text: A very early member of Mynet, the Italian ISP Association, Mynet actively advocates for open access, digital sovereignty and fair competition in the EU telecom market. With deep roots and forward-looking ambition, the company embodies the European values of innovation, inclusion and autonomy.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Introductory Note
paragraphs.text: Due to the complexity of the issues at stake and the limitations of the 4,000-character constraint, we were unable to adequately convey our position within the standard submission form. For this reason, we have attached a dedicated document that provides a more thorough and accurate elaboration of our views.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
1. Focus enforcement on Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs)
paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act should apply strict and permanent oversight to Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) as defined under the Digital Services Act. These players have systemic influence over users, particularly vulnerable groups, and concentrate data, visibility, and revenue.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
1. Focus enforcement on Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs)
paragraphs.text: We strongly recommend that VLOPs be subject to continuous monitoring by a dedicated European authority, with enhanced transparency and auditing obligations.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
1. Focus enforcement on Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs)
paragraphs.text: For all other players, particularly SMEs, enforcement should occur only in response to formal complaints. SMEs should not be subject to continuous or pre-emptive control mechanisms.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: To avoid disproportionate regulatory burdens, the Commission should adopt a two-tier compliance model based on business size:

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: SMEs and micro-enterprises should be allowed to self-certify compliance using a simplified, cost-free procedure that does not require external consultants or expensive certification bodies.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: Any sanctioning mechanism must include:

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: minimum impact thresholds below which no enforcement is triggered,

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: prior warnings and time-limited correction windows,

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: and penalty scaling based on business size and intent.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
2. Self-certification and proportional obligations for SMEs
paragraphs.text: This approach promotes trust and alignment without penalizing good-faith operators.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
3. EU-funded open-source toolkit and crowd-sourced reporting
paragraphs.text: To ensure widespread and cost-effective compliance:

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
3. EU-funded open-source toolkit and crowd-sourced reporting
paragraphs.text: The Commission should fund and maintain a shared open-source library of UX flows, notification templates, consent interfaces, and cancellation procedures that are compliant by design. This will allow SMEs to implement best practices without legal or technical uncertainty.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
3. EU-funded open-source toolkit and crowd-sourced reporting
paragraphs.text: A crowd-sourced reporting platform should be established at EU level, open to both consumers and businesses, to report and flag truly unfair practices by market players. This platform should feed enforcement actions and reduce reliance on expensive supervisory structures.

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
3. EU-funded open-source toolkit and crowd-sourced reporting

section_path: Mynet Contribution to the Consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
3. EU-funded open-source toolkit and crowd-sourced reporting
paragraphs.text: Mynet Uso pubblico

section_path: 4. Areas where intervention is genuinely helpful
paragraphs.text: Mynet supports intervention in the following critical areas, where unfair practices are rampant and affect both consumers and honest businesses:

section_path: 4. Areas where intervention is genuinely helpful
paragraphs.text: Deceptive pricing techniques, including manipulative dynamic pricing, fake discounting, and "from X" misleading offers.

section_path: 4. Areas where intervention is genuinely helpful
paragraphs.text: Undisclosed influencer marketing, particularly involving health-related or harmful products, with joint responsibility for the platforms and sponsor companies.

section_path: 4. Areas where intervention is genuinely helpful
paragraphs.text: Unclear subscriptions, where free trials convert into paid contracts without proper notice or with hidden cancellation paths.

section_path: 4. Areas where intervention is genuinely helpful
paragraphs.text: Addictive design practices targeting minors, especially in online games and platforms, that exploit behavioral vulnerabilities for monetization.

section_path: 5. Avoid regulatory overlap and legal fragmentation
paragraphs.text: Many of the issues identified in this initiative are already partially addressed under existing regulations (DSA, DMA, GDPR, AVMSD, AI Act). Mynet warns against regulatory overreach or duplication:

section_path: 5. Avoid regulatory overlap and legal fragmentation
paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act should be a complementary and harmonized tool, not a source of additional complexity.

section_path: 5. Avoid regulatory overlap and legal fragmentation
paragraphs.text: Enforcement mechanisms, reporting platforms and legal definitions must be convergent, interoperable and easy to understand for SMEs operating across the EU.

section_path: Final remarks
paragraphs.text: A well-crafted Digital Fairness Act has the potential to clean up harmful practices in the online economy and restore trust for both consumers and honest entrepreneurs. However, its success depends on targeted enforcement, proportionality, legal clarity, and support mechanisms for smaller market actors. We urge the Commission to ensure that the final legislation distinguishes clearly between systemic abusers and the millions of compliant SMEs that form the backbone of the European digital economy.

section_path: Final remarks
paragraphs.text: Mynet remains available to participate in further technical discussions and provide the perspective of SME operators and independent ISPs across Europe.

section_path: Final remarks
